title: Verbesina standleyi
taxonomy: Verbesina standleyi is a species of flowering plant in the family Asteraceae. It is native to Central and South America, particularly in countries such as Mexico and Guatemala. The plant is also known as a member of the genus Verbesina, which includes over 100 species of flowering plants. Verbesina standleyi is often classified under the order Asterales, a group of flowering plants that includes sunflowers and daisies. As a member of the Asteraceae family, Verbesina standleyi is closely related to other plants with similar characteristics and growth habits.
uses: Verbesina standleyi is a species of flowering plant that has been traditionally used in some cultures for its medicinal properties. Its uses include treating various health issues, such as fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, due to the presence of bioactive compounds. These compounds have been shown to possess anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties. The sesquiterpene lactones, in particular, have been found to exhibit cytotoxic and immunosuppressive activities. intro: Verbesina standleyi, also known as Stilte palo, is a plant species native to Mexico and Central America, belonging to the daisy family (Asteraceae). The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, inflammation, and respiratory issues, due to its potential anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Verbesina standleyi include flavonoids, alkaloids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The plant is often used to make herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and extracts, which are commonly used to treat coughs, colds, and other respiratory ailments.
